title: Layout Managers, Resize, Scaling, High DPI, and Reflow · Runtime Lens: Display Scaling
summary: The Canvas has fixed pixels, controls vanish on a small window, pack and grid conflict in one parent, and high-DPI text clips. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the display scaling increment in responsive desktop layout and resize policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Test Tk scaling, font metrics, line widths, focus indicators, and platform display settings instead of assuming raw pixels.
Handle configure events with bounded coalescing and redraw only from committed current dimensions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example puzzle team handles case 4748 during a clean desktop release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to derive a responsive cell scale while retaining visible controls and valid geometry; all graph claims need an independent witness; then change display scale and retain focus and control access. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. A defensible solution applies Display Scaling, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to responsive desktop layout and resize policy.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-layout-resize-scaling-2
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-layout-resize-scaling-2.
# Case 7417: a keyboard-only puzzle tournament.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant.
def worked_maze_layout_resize_scaling_2(available_width: int = 720, available_height: int = 540, grid_extent: int = 18, cell_size: int = 28, visible_controls: int = 7) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "gridworked2",
        "topology7417",
        "search2dekrow",
        "eventworked27417",
        "access7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if min(available_width, available_height, grid_extent, cell_size) < 1:
        return False, cell_size, "layout-shape"
    if cell_size * grid_extent > min(available_width, available_height):
        return False, cell_size, "viewport-overflow"
    if visible_controls < 5:
        return False, visible_controls, "control-clipping"
    if grid_extent > 512:
        return False, grid_extent, "resize-budget"
    assert cell_size * grid_extent <= min(available_width, available_height)
    return True, cell_size, "responsive-maze-layout"

# Evidence: maze-display-scaling-q2w8oe

def evidence_maze_display_scaling_q2w8oe(available_width: int = 720, available_height: int = 540, grid_extent: int = 18, cell_size: int = 28, visible_controls: int = 7) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_q2w8oe = "q2w8oe-eo8w2q-6177"

    evidence_axes_q2w8oe = (

        "topology6177",
        "searcheo8w2q",
        "eventq2w8oe6177",
        "access6177q2w8oe",

        return False, grid_extent, "resize-budget"
    assert cell_size * grid_extent <= min(available_width, available_height)
    return True, cell_size, "responsive-maze-layout"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test Tk scaling, font metrics, line widths, focus indicators, and platform display settings instead of assuming raw pixels.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Display Scaling model · Theory Display Scaling, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Display Scaling precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Display Scaling model · Theory Display Scaling, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 2938 during a museum kiosk accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to derive a responsive cell scale while retaining visible controls and valid geometry; native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution; then replace pointer input with a single-action keyboard path. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Test Tk scaling, font metrics, line widths, focus indicators, and platform display settings instead of assuming raw pixels.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Display Scaling model · Theory Display Scaling, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: High DPI only affects image assets.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: maze-display-scaling-q2w8oe.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: maze-display-scaling-q2w8oe(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_maze_display_scaling_q2w8oe\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?min\(available_width, available_height, grid_extent, cell_size\) < 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?cell_size \* grid_extent > min\(available_width, available_height\))(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?visible_controls < 5)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?grid_extent > 512)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
